issues.comment: r.log.Error() is used for routine informational logging in annotation cleanup operations. This is wrong for multiple reasons: (1) Error level will trigger monitoring alerts and create log noise on every cleanup cycle. (2) The 'err' field is logged after a nil check, so err is always nil — misleading in an error-level log. (3) Full ID slices (up to AnnotationCleanupJobBatchSize entries, e.g. 32,767 IDs) are logged every batch iteration, creating enormous log entries. These should be at Debug level or removed entirely.

Cleanup ticker reduced from 10 minutes to 1 minute, but the hardcoded 9-minute timeout in clean() at line 123 was designed as a pair with the 10-minute interval (ensuring cleanup finishes before the next tick). With a 1-minute ticker, if any cleanup cycle takes >1 minute, the next cycle fires immediately after. The 9-minute timeout still applies, so cleanup could run back-to-back for up to 9 minutes, generating 10x the expected database load. This appears to be a debugging leftover that should be reverted to 10 minutes.
